title: Trump Is Finally Eyeing an Exit From Iran. But Will He Take It?
summary: President Trump says he is considering “winding down” operations in Iran. But many of his original war goals remain unaccomplished.

title: A Look at the Escalating Battle for the Strait of Hormuz
summary: There are several ways the United States could attempt to reopen the waterway, all of which carry substantial risks.

title: Internet Blackout Keeps Iranians From Reaching Loved Ones During Nowruz Holiday
summary: The current restrictions on the internet in Iran are coinciding with Nowruz, the observance of the Persian New Year.

title: Robert Mueller, Former FBI Director Who Led Trump Inquiry, Dies at 81
summary: He imposed the most significant overhaul of the F.B.I. in its history. After concluding that Russia had interfered in the 2016 election, he became a target of the president’s anger.

title: What Was the Mueller Investigation? And What Did It Find?
summary: The appointment of Robert S. Mueller III as special counsel to conduct an investigation related to a sitting president roiled Washington around the start of President Trump’s first term.

title: ICE Agents to Be Sent to U.S. Airports on Monday, Trump Says
summary: The president issued a threat to deploy ICE agents in an apparent attempt to force Democrats to approve a new budget for the Department of Homeland Security.

title: How Corey Lewandowski Wielded Power Inside D.H.S.
summary: While he was supposed to serve in a limited role as an adviser to Kristi Noem, Mr. Lewandowski had wide influence over contracts, personnel and operations inside the agency over the last year.

title: News Outlets Pressure Pentagon to Restore Access After Court Ruling
summary: A federal judge ruled on Friday that key parts of the Pentagon’s press policy were unconstitutional.

title: Trump’s Contested Campus Antisemitism Fight Is Accelerating Again
summary: While some Jewish students and faculty members have applauded the Trump administration’s renewed push against antisemitism, others believe the government has different motives.

title: No Pills or Needles, Just Paper: How Deadly Drugs Are Changing
summary: Lab-made drugs soaked into the pages of letters, books and even legal documents are being smuggled behind bars, killing inmates and frustrating investigators.

title: CBS News Radio Crackles to a Close
summary: A stalwart of the mass media century, the longstanding network that launched the careers of journalists like Edward R. Murrow will air its final broadcast in May.

title: Let-It-Rip Jeremy vs. Sneaky Sam
summary: Nothing artificial about this Hollywood A.I. smackdown.

title: Jay Manuel: What Tyra Banks and I Should Have Known
summary: For years, Jay Manuel helped create reality television. Now, he finds himself on the other side of the production.

title: Already Flooded, Oahu Braces for More Rain
summary: Thousands of residents on Oahu had yet to return home as the Hawaiian island dealt with the aftermath of severe rainfall. The storm was moving south over the island toward Maui.

title: You’ve Lost Your Health Insurance. It Shouldn’t Have Been a Surprise.
summary: You would think you’d get texts, emails, phone calls and letters if your insurer was about to drop you. But you may be wrong.
